title: Translation of {a SPARQL SELECT query involving BIND, conditional-or, and multiple EXISTS} produces a SQL syntax error
description:
Version
Virtuoso version 08.03.3323 on Linux (x86_64-generic-linux-glibc25) Single Server Edition (62 GB total memory, 44 GB memory in use)

How to reproduce

Open https://dbpedia.org/sparql/ in a browser.
Replace the content of the "Query Text" text area with this:
SELECT * WHERE { BIND ((EXISTS {} || EXISTS {}) AS ?a) }


Click "Execute Query".

Actual result
Virtuoso 37000 Error SQ074: Line 8: syntax error at 'EXISTS' before '('

Expected result
1
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id Int32, name String, value Float64, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now());
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Test A', 10.5), (2, 'Test B', NULL), (3, 'Test C', 20.0);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE (countIf(value IS NOT NULL) > 0 OR countIf(name = 'Test B') > 0) AND value > 15.0;
UPDATE test_table SET value = 25.0 WHERE id = 3;
SELECT (countIf(value > 15.0) > 0 OR countIf(name = 'Test A') > 0) AS a FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: BIND with conditional-or and multiple EXISTS in SPARQL
